steps.title: Identify Bremner's claim
steps.content: In Text 1, Bremner argues that Appia lacked technical expertise in the use of light in the theater.

steps.title: Find Text 2's counter
steps.content: Text 2 directly rejects this by saying Appia was "not an amateur" and points to his apprenticeship with a lighting specialist, where he observed portable lighting devices firsthand. That apprenticeship is the source of his technical expertise.

steps.title: Eliminate per choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'lighting technicians dismissed Appia's ideas': Text 2 never mentions technicians reacting to Appia; this is unsupported overreach. ✗
  • Choice B — 'gained a level of technical expertise during his apprenticeship': directly mirrors Text 2's argument and rebuts Bremner's claim. ✓
  • Choice C — 'practitioners who worked with Appia greatly admired his work': Text 2 says nothing about admiration from collaborators; off topic. ✗
  • Choice D — 'unfamiliar with the use of music and sound': the disagreement is about lighting, not music; introduces an irrelevant topic. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
